page: UpCloud 1xCPU-2GB is an entry-level virtual server built on the KVM hypervisor. It features a single shared vCPU mapped to one physical core of an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz. The system architecture is x86_64, supported by 2.0 GB of system memory and 50 GB of local SSD storage. The CPU cache configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 0.5 MB of L2 cache, and 16 MB of L3 cache. It also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark evaluations indicate that this server is positioned at the lower end of the performance spectrum. The stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance scores are 737.37, placing it in the bottom 10% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with read-only large block speeds at 9.32 GB/sec and write speeds at 7.37 GB/sec. However, single-threaded bzip3 decompression achieves an average tier result of 8.82 GB/sec. Redis SET operations reach 1.4 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is measured at 1.12 GB/sec.

Given its shared CPU allocation and modest memory capacity, this server offers a highly cost-efficient entry point for basic hosting needs where dedicated CPU resources are not required. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU model, which can introduce performance variability under sustained loads. This configuration is best suited for lightweight workloads such as basic web serving, development environments, small utility scripts, and low-traffic database testing.
description: UpCloud 1xCPU-2GB is an entry-level, budget-friendly virtual server utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with a single shared vCPU from an AMD EPYC 7542 processor running at 2.0 GHz, paired with 2.0 GB of RAM and 50 GB of local SSD storage. Built on the x86_64 architecture, this instance is designed for low-intensity applications. Benchmark results place its CPU and memory performance largely in the bottom 10%, with a stress-ng score of 737.37 and a static web serving throughput of 1.12 GB/sec. However, it maintains a stable 1.4 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks and an average single-threaded bzip3 decompression speed of 8.82 GB/sec. This server represents a highly cost-efficient option for lightweight workloads, making it suitable for basic web serving, development environments, and small utility tasks.
og_description: UpCloud 1xCPU-2GB is an entry-level virtual server featuring a shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPU, 2.0 GB of RAM, and 50 GB of local SSD storage, designed for lightweight utility workloads and basic web serving.
meta_description: UpCloud 1xCPU-2GB is a budget-friendly virtual server with 1 shared AMD EPYC vCPU, 2.0 GB RAM, and 50 GB SSD storage for basic workloads.
tagline: A budget-friendly, shared-core virtual server with local SSD storage designed for lightweight hosting and development environments.

2.0 GB of system memory and 50 GB of local SSD storage
Delivers 1.4 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks
Best suited for lightweight web serving and development environments
